content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
May 25, 1989 
Lynne Andersen 
Legislative Assistant 
Assemblyman William J. Filante 
State capitol 
P.O. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Dear Ms. Andersen: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-273 
We have received your request for advice regarding the mass 
mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") ,1 as 
amended by Proposition 73 on the June 7, 1988 ballot. 
QUESTION 
Does the sample postcard, which Assemblyman Filante uses to 
notify his constituents about assembly district mobile office 
hours, fall within an exception to the mass mailing provisions? 
CONCLUSION 
Neither the text nor the purpose of the sample postcard falls 
within an exception to the mass mailing provisions. 
Regulation 
18901(f) (8) only allows elected officials to notify their 
constituents of public meetings, not district office hours. 
FACTS 
In the past, Assemblyman Filante has used postcards to notify 
a large number of his constituents about assembly district mobile 
office hours, which are held periodically throughout the year. 
The postcard is sent to each household in a certain zip code. 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


File No. A-89-273 
Page 2 
The sample postcard, which you submitted for our review, 
contains the following message: 
ASSEMBLYMAN 
BILL FILANTE, M.D. 
Invites you to 
Come and visit 
his 9th A.D. Mobile Office. 
Take this opportunity to discuss 
any problems you may have or 
share your views on important' 
state and local issues. 
Bill will be at: 
Long's Drug Store 
1707 Grant Avenue 
Novato 
12 p.m. to 2 p.m. 
Saturday, April 22, 1989 
For more information call (415) 479-4920 
The postcard also contains a photograph of Assemblyman Filante and 
the seal of the State Assembly. 
The return address on the back of the postcard contains As-
semblyman Filante's name, office, and address. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 89001 of the Political Reform Act, as amended by 
Proposition 73, provides that "no newsletter or other mass mailing 
shall be sent at public expense." 
A mass mailing is defined in 
Section 82041.5 as two hundred or more substantially similar 
pieces of mail. 
The Commission has determined that the intent of 
the voters in adopting Proposition 73 was to prevent elected of-
ficers from gaining an advantage from incumbency by using pUblic 
funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings which 
increase their name recognition. 
At its December 6, 1988 meeting, the Commission adopted 
Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed), which implements Section 89001. 
Regulation 18901(a) provides: 
(a) 
Except as provided herein, a newsletter 
or other mass mailing is "sent at public expense" 
within the meaning of Government Code Section 89001 
if any of the costs of design, production, printing 
or distribution, is paid for with public moneys as 
defined in Government Code Section 85102(e). 
Regulation 18901(c) states that a newsletter or other mass 
mailing may not be sent at pUblic expense if: 


File No. A-89-273 
Page 3 
(1) 
The name of the elected officer or his or 
her photograph appears on the document; and 
(A) 
The elected officer exercises direc-
tion and control over the content, production, 
or distribution of the document, or 
(B) 
The document is sent at the request 
or suggestion of the elected officer or his or 
her agent; or 
(C) 
The document is signed by, or is 
designated as being from, the elected officer 
or his or her office; or 
(2) 
(A) 
The elected officer is affiliated 
with the agency which produces or distributes the 
document; and 
(i) 
The elected officer is featured in 
the document; or 
(ii) 
The name, office or other reference 
to the elected officer or his or her 
photograph appears on the document and the 
document is prepared or sent in cooperation, 
consultation, coordination or concert with the 
elected officer. 
(B) 
An elected officer is "featured" in 
a mass mailing if he or she is singled out for 
attention of the reader by use of his or her 
signature, inclusion in any photograph, or the 
manner of display of his or her name or office 
in the layout of the document such as by 
headlines, type size, or typeface. 
Regulation 18901(f) lists several exceptions to the general 
rule that no newsletter or mass mailing shall be sent at public 
expense. 
However, all exceptions to the mass mailing provisions 
are to be construed narrowly. 
(See Estate of Banerjee (1978) 21 
Cal. 3d 527, 540.) 
For example, Regulation 18901(f) (8) provides 
that: 
(f) 
The following newsletters or other mass 
mailings are not prohibited by Government Code Sec-
tion 89001 if the mailing is sent to the persons 
specified in each instance below and the mailing 
consists of: 
* * * 


File No. A-89-273 
Page 4 
(8) 
Mailings sent to the elected 
officer's constituents which directly relate 
to that elected official's incumbent 
governmental duties and which solely include 
the time, date, place, and a concise descrip-
tion of the subject matter of a public meeting 
to be held by the elected officer; (Emphasis 
added. ) 
To apply this exception, the mailing must directly relate to 
the elected official's incumbent governmental duties, and may only 
include the time, date, place, and a concise description of the 
subject matter of the meeting. 
The name of the elected officer 
may be used once in the notice in order to identify the elected 
officer holding the meeting. 
However, the mailing may not be 
signed by the officer. 
(Clark Advice Letter No. 1-89-031, copy 
enclosed. ) 
As can be seen, this exception pertains specifically to 
"public meetings ll and not to district office hours. 
The purpose 
of the exception is to allow elected officials to notify their 
constituents of public meetings. 
In reviewing the sample 
postcard, it appears that neither the purpose nor the text 
complies with the restrictions of the regulation. 
Since the 
exception is to be construed narrowly, Assemblyman Filante's 
sample postcard may not be utiliZed. 
Assemblyman Filante may use a postcard to notify his 
constituents of a public meeting pursuant to Regulation 
18901(f) (8), however he may not be "featured" by use of his 
photograph, signature, or display of his name in large type size. 
(Regulation 18901 (c) (2) (B).) 
He may use his name once in the 
description of the meeting. 
For example, the notice may say "As-
semblyman Filante invites you to a community meeting", as long as 
that is the only reference to himself in the mailing. 
The state-
ment "for more information call", followed by the district office 
telephone number is not permitted. 
(Connelly Advice Letter No. 1-
89-110, 111 and 112, copy enclosed.) 
If Assemblyman Filante uses a postcard that complies with the 
requirements of Regulation 18901(f) (8), another exception governs 
the use of an elected official's name as part of the return 
address on a postcard or envelope. 
Regulation 18901(e) 
establishes an exception if certain criteria are met. 
It states: 
(e) 
A newsletter or other mass mailing is not 
prohibited by Government Code Section 89001 if it 
meets all of the following criteria: 
(1) The stationery, forms and envelopes 
used for the mailing are the standard 
stationery, forms and envelopes of the agency 
or committee of the agency; and 


File No. A-89-273 
Page 5 
(2) The name of an elected officer who is 
affiliated with the agency or committee ap-
pears, in the standard letterhead or logotype 
of the stationery, forms or envelopes of the 
agency, a committee of the agency, or the 
elected official and the newsletter or mass 
mailing is not otherwise prohibited under 
subdivision (c). 
As used in this subdivision, the term "let-
terhead or logotype" includes a listing of agency 
or committee officials or members, in which all who 
are listed appear in the same typeface and type 
size and location in the layout of the newsletter 
or other mass mailing. 
As used in this subdivision, the term 
"standard letterhead or logotype" refers to any 
regularly used by the agency, subunit or the 
elected officer .... 
Therefore, the postcard must be the "standard" form used by 
your office and the return address must be the "standard let-
terhead or logotype" regularly used. 
If such standard letterhead 
is regularly used by your office, Assemblyman Filante's name may 
be included in the return address. 
As stated above, all excep-
tions to the mass mailing provisions are to be construed narrowly. 
I hope this answers your questions regarding the mass mailing 
provisions as they pertain to meeting notices. 
If you have any 
questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:JRS:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
~
tLl£. ;Jultw 
Jillr R. Stecher 
Cou' el, Legal Division

WILLIAM J. FILANTE, M.D. 
JOHN MCLEAN 
ASSEMBLYMAN. NINTH DISTRICT 
(MARIN AND SONOMA COUNTIES) 
LYNNE ANDERSEN, LEGISLATIVE ASSISTANT 
MAY 5, 1989 
MOBILE DISTRICT OFFICE MAILING 
COMM1TTEE:S: 
VICE CHAIRMAN: 
RULES

John, attached is a card that has previously been sent to 
announce mobile district office hours in the district. 
It 
has been sent to each household in a certain zip code. 
This year, it has been approved for mailing by the 
Legislative Counsel under Prop. 73 regulations. 
However, I 
would also like an opinion from the FPPC. 
If the mailing is not under Prop. 73 regulations, please 
forward any changes that can be made to make it comply. 
Should you have any questions, please contact me at 5-7827. 
Thank you for your consideration. 
lIra 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
May 9, 1989 
Lynne Andersen 
Legislative Assistant 
William J. Filante, M.D. 
Assemblyman, Ninth District 
state Capitol 
P.o. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-273 
Dear Ms. Andersen: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on May 8, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Jill Stecher an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916)~322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660

Does the sample postcard, which Assemblyman Filante uses to 
notify his constituents about assembly district mobile office 
hours, fall within an exception to the mass mailing provisions?

Neither the text nor the purpose of the sample postcard falls 
within an exception to the mass mailing provisions. 
Regulation 
18901(f) (8) only allows elected officials to notify their 
constituents of public meetings, not district office hours.

QUESTION: Does the sample postcard, which Assemblyman Filante uses to 
notify his constituents about assembly district mobile office 
hours, fall within an exception to the mass mailing provisions?

CONCLUSION: Neither the text nor the purpose of the sample postcard falls 
within an exception to the mass mailing provisions. 
Regulation 
18901(f) (8) only allows elected officials to notify their 
constituents of public meetings, not district office hours.
